danger_signals.visual_form: cyborg agents with distinctive visual loadouts matching their faction (Tao, Sphinx Inc., Castrilos)
danger_signals.meaning: immediate combat threat; territory under enemy control

danger_signals.visual_form: uniformed law enforcement entities in contested or neutral territories
danger_signals.meaning: territory not yet under player control; resistance to takeover

danger_signals.visual_form: civilians with weapons in over-taxed territories
danger_signals.meaning: revolt condition; imminent loss of territory if not addressed

danger_signals.visual_form: high concentration of hostile entities on screen during Atlantic Accelerator finale
danger_signals.meaning: escalating threat intensity; endgame challenge state

safety_signals.visual_form: isometric terrain zones displaying player faction color scheme
safety_signals.meaning: secure zones under player syndicate tax revenue generation

safety_signals.visual_form: player-controlled cyborg agents clustered together with unified visual identity
civilians or persuaded NPCs following agent command paths
safety_signals.meaning: successful Persuadertron use; population control achieved

faction_indicators.color_identity: player-assigned customizable syndicate color in territory overlay
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: corporation branding in HUD and control airship
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: controlled territories display faction color taxation zones on isometric map

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: rival syndicate branding on agent armor/loadouts
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: cyborg agent visual appearance distinct from player agents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: territories under Tao control show faction territorial color

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: rival syndicate branding on agent armor/loadouts
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: cyborg agent visual appearance distinct from player and Tao agents
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: territories under Sphinx Inc. control show faction territorial color

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: rival syndicate branding on agent armor/loadouts
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: cyborg agent visual appearance distinct from other factions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: territories under Castrilos control show faction territorial color

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: police insignia on units and patrol vehicles
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: uniformed police differ from cyborg agents by biological appearance and armor style
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: presence indicates uncontrolled or early-stage conquest territories

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: territorial color shifts to player syndicate color; police presence remains but weakened
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: reduced enemy resistance; police forces retreat or are eliminated; tax revenue begins flowing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: visual stress indicators on terrain; civilian density appears agitated or armed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: civilians exhibit aggressive stance; armed revolt units spawn; agents required to suppress

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: fractured or flickering territorial color overlay; heavy armed civilian presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: loss of tax revenue; mission replay required to re-secure; civilians actively resist player agents

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: most territories display player faction color; endgame location (Atlantic Accelerator) shows maximum enemy concentration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: final wave of rival syndicate elite agents; faction elimination sequences trigger

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: wounded/low-health agents display visual damage indicators or reduced animation frames
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: agents move slower; reduced combat effectiveness; medikits required to restore function

environmental_storytelling.example: isometric map zones colored by controlling syndicate; visual dominance hierarchy shows which factions hold which cities
contested territories show mixed police and rival agent presence; heavily defended areas signal strategic importance
research stations, corporate headquarters, and holding facilities visually marked on isometric grid; architecture hints at objective type
agent weaponry visibly changes on-screen (pistol → uzi → minigun → flamethrower); escalating destruction communicates technological advancement
agent visual models change as cybernetic enhancements are researched and applied; armor plating, weapon hardpoints become visible upgrades
isometric cityscape shows cramped, dense environments under corporate control; visual monotony of architecture reinforces megacorp dominance narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: follows pathfinding commands; weapon selected determines movement speed and stance; executes targeted attacks or persuasion actions
pursues player agents with tactical formation; returns fire; seeks cover in urban terrain; coordinated multi-agent tactics
patrols assigned zones; engages player agents with standard firearms; retreats if overwhelmed; lower tactical coordination than syndicate agents
wanders aimlessly on streets; avoids active combat zones; pedestrian traffic patterns suggest urban life
follows player agent commands; can be directed as cannon fodder; used in assassination or capture missions
armed civilians actively attack player agents; occupy streets in dense groups; aggressive engagement pattern indicates political instability

notes: Syndicate (Genesis 1994) uses isometric real-time tactics perspective to visualize corporate territorial dominance through color-coded map zones. Visual communication of world-state relies heavily on territorial UI coloring, NPC density, and agent appearance modifications. The cyberpunk dystopian setting is communicated through dense urban architecture and the presence of cyborg agents themselves as manifestations of corporate body-modification control. Faction identity is primarily signaled through enemy agent armor visual variants and controlled territory color overlays rather than explicit sigils or symbols. The game's political situation (corporate megacorp dominance, civilian control through CHIP implants) is conveyed narratively in the manual rather than dynamically in-game, though over-taxation mechanics and revolt behavior provide indirect political state visualization.
